message:
chore(beads): reopen franken_numpy-4jlw — parallel-test flake persists after AttrGuard

AttrGuard landed in 782994a but 3 tests (count_nonzero_*, frompyfunc_live_*)
still flake under parallel 'cargo test -p fnp-python --lib' even though
382/382 pass under --test-threads=1. Need deeper investigation of pyo3
class-registration state, not just numpy.setattr save/restore.

files.patch:
@@ -103,7 +103,7 @@
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4g5","title":"Validate and land live fnp-ufunc integer-sidecar + datetime differential fixes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:18:02.858801190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:21:09.047109428Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:21:09.046230794Z","close_reason":"Validated live shared-worktree ufunc/datetime changes; targeted RCH checks passed; remaining workspace fmt/clippy blockers split to follow-up bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"franken_numpy-4g5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Verification so far: rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets = pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-conformance datetime_differential -- --nocapture = pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p fnp-ufunc integer_sidecar -- --nocapture = pass. cargo fmt --check is currently blocked by unrelated pre-existing formatting drift outside bead scope (fnp-conformance/bin, fnp-conformance/src/lib.rs, fnp-io, fnp-linalg/tests, fnp-random).","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:20:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4gxm","title":"numpy.broadcast_shapes python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.broadcast_shapes passthrough wrapper around numpy.broadcast_shapes(*args). Include oracle test covering: pairwise shape broadcast, more than two input shapes, scalar-empty shape inputs, integer arguments accepted as 1-D shapes where numpy permits them, large rank alignment, and incompatible shapes raising ValueError with numpy-matching surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:18:21Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:37:40.906574250Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:37:40.904738721Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — wrapper delivered in commit 8e55b7a; lib.rs exposes fn and oracle-parity test is committed (verified via git log grep br-franken_numpy-4gxm).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4hw","title":"Verify fnp-ndarray reshape ambiguity and stride-calculus surface","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T05:23:22.045390575Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-27T05:25:12.615862845Z","closed_at":"2026-03-27T05:25:12.615609335Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"franken_numpy-4jlw","title":"[HIGH] fnp-python lib tests flaky under parallel test threads — numpy state pollution","description":"Three fnp-python lib tests fail intermittently when run with cargo's default parallel test threads. They all pass reliably with '--test-threads=1'.

Affected tests:
- tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_axis_none            (left: 'np.int64(3)', right: '3')
- tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_keepdims_none_and_scalar_inputs (left: 'np.int64(0)', right: '0')
- tests::frompyfunc_live_callable_matches_numpy_reduce_scalar_where_and_direct_out (left/right swap between 'output must be an array' and 'return arrays must be of ArrayType')

Reproducer:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib               # 3 FAILED
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib -- --test-threads=1  # 380 PASSED

Suspected cause: multiple pyo3 tests sharing the embedded CPython interpreter across threads. Symptoms include 'fnp_python::PyOGridClass is unsendable, but is being dropped on another thread' RuntimeErrors printed during the failing run. Possible contributors:
- tests::frompyfunc_reduce_does_not_delegate_to_numpy_frompyfunc poisons numpy.frompyfunc via setattr and restores it at end — if another test runs in parallel while numpy.frompyfunc is poisoned, the global replacement leaks.
- Custom PyClass instances registered by early tests linger in numpy's object cache.

Priority [HIGH]: Not a correctness bug but blocks clean 'cargo test --workspace' runs; CI almost certainly flakes on these three. Fix direction:
1. (Quick) Document --test-threads=1 requirement in crates/fnp-python/README.md or Cargo.toml [[bin]] entry.
2. (Better) Move the poison/restore dance in frompyfunc_reduce_does_not_delegate_to_numpy_frompyfunc into a process-exclusive section via a Mutex<()> shared across the three affected tests.
3. (Best) Rework tests that depend on numpy state comparisons to NOT use 'with_python' across threads; use a per-test embedded interpreter.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:13:56.755640202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T22:26:51.125213558Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T22:26:51.122986250Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"franken_numpy-4jlw","title":"[HIGH] fnp-python lib tests flaky under parallel test threads — numpy state pollution","description":"Three fnp-python lib tests fail intermittently when run with cargo's default parallel test threads. They all pass reliably with '--test-threads=1'.

Affected tests:
- tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_axis_none            (left: 'np.int64(3)', right: '3')
- tests::count_nonzero_matches_numpy_keepdims_none_and_scalar_inputs (left: 'np.int64(0)', right: '0')
- tests::frompyfunc_live_callable_matches_numpy_reduce_scalar_where_and_direct_out (left/right swap between 'output must be an array' and 'return arrays must be of ArrayType')

Reproducer:
  export CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib               # 3 FAILED
  cargo test -p fnp-python --lib -- --test-threads=1  # 380 PASSED

Suspected cause: multiple pyo3 tests sharing the embedded CPython interpreter across threads. Symptoms include 'fnp_python::PyOGridClass is unsendable, but is being dropped on another thread' RuntimeErrors printed during the failing run. Possible contributors:
- tests::frompyfunc_reduce_does_not_delegate_to_numpy_frompyfunc poisons numpy.frompyfunc via setattr and restores it at end — if another test runs in parallel while numpy.frompyfunc is poisoned, the global replacement leaks.
- Custom PyClass instances registered by early tests linger in numpy's object cache.

Priority [HIGH]: Not a correctness bug but blocks clean 'cargo test --workspace' runs; CI almost certainly flakes on these three. Fix direction:
1. (Quick) Document --test-threads=1 requirement in crates/fnp-python/README.md or Cargo.toml [[bin]] entry.
2. (Better) Move the poison/restore dance in frompyfunc_reduce_does_not_delegate_to_numpy_frompyfunc into a process-exclusive section via a Mutex<()> shared across the three affected tests.
3. (Best) Rework tests that depend on numpy state comparisons to NOT use 'with_python' across threads; use a per-test embedded interpreter.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T22:13:56.755640202Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:14:40.813335724Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4ln6","title":"Add hsplit/vsplit/dsplit convenience functions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-01T17:32:49.767905532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:34:42.202565864Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:34:42.202192397Z","close_reason":"hsplit/vsplit/dsplit already existed. Dropped duplicate definitions. The split_at variant was added in the prior bead.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4ln8","title":"numpy.fft: rfft2 python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.rfft2 passthrough wrapper around numpy.fft.rfft2(a, s=None, axes=(-2, -1), norm=None, out=None). 2-D real-input FFT. Include oracle test covering: 2-D real input default, explicit s shape, axes=(0, 1) explicit, norm='ortho'/'forward', verify last-axis output is n//2+1, complex dtype of output, and irfft2 round-trip recovery.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:49:31.728415827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:25.418813286Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T12:04:25.416932758Z","close_reason":"Closed by cc-numpy — numpy.fft.rfft2 passthrough wrapper added to fnp-python in commit 59430e9. Parity test covers 4x4 arange default with output shape (4, 3) verification (last axis = n//2+1 for n=4), axes=(0,1) norm='ortho'. rch test: 1/1 passed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_numpy-4mqw","title":"numpy.argsort python wrapper parity","description":"Add fnp_python.argsort passthrough wrapper around numpy.argsort(a, axis=-1, kind=None, order=None, *, stable=None). Include oracle test covering: 1-D integer input, axis=None flattening behavior, axis=0/1 on 2-D input, structured dtype ordering via order=..., stable=True parity on tied values, and NaN/complex ordering surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:12:20Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T06:26:25Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T06:26:25Z","close_reason":"Completed br-franken_numpy-4mqw: numpy.argsort parity was already present on main","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
